rulings.Case Title: CANDICE CLARK, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Plaintiff Candice Clarks Responses To Rscc Wire Cable Llcs Request For Admissions Admitted And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, November 28, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1, Defendant RSCC Wire & Cable LLC¿s motion to deem plaintiff Candice Clark¿s responses to RSCC Wire & Cable¿s request for admissions admitted and request for sanctions is denied as moot. Plaintiffs served responses prior to the hearing on this motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SHIFFER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, November 28, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2, Defendant General Electric Co.¿s motion for protective order: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
